items.headline: Claude's privacy policy now lets Anthropic share your chats with police, no warrant required
items.summary: Anthropic's updated privacy policy took effect July 8: on Free, Pro, and Max plans (not Enterprise, Team, or API), the company can now hand your Claude conversations to law enforcement whenever it has a "good faith belief" that's warranted, without waiting for a subpoena or court order. Separately, a small number of accounts flagged for possible policy violations may now be asked to submit a government ID and a selfie for a facial scan to keep using Claude.
items.why_it_matters: If you use a personal Claude plan for brainstorming, drafts, or business notes, treat it like email rather than a private diary, since Anthropic itself now decides when your conversations get shared with authorities.

opportunity.action_note: If you're on a Free, Pro, or Max Claude plan, read the updated privacy policy today and stop typing anything into Claude you wouldn't want shared with law enforcement without a court order.
opportunity.angle: I broke down what Claude's new privacy policy actually means before you keep typing into it

items.headline: Elon Musk's SpaceXAI launches Grok 4.5, claims it rivals Claude's top model
items.summary: SpaceXAI (formerly xAI) released Grok 4.5 on July 8, its first model since folding into SpaceX and buying the coding tool Cursor. Musk calls it "Opus-class," meaning roughly as capable as Anthropic's top Claude model, but faster and cheaper at $2 per million input tokens and $6 per million output tokens.
items.why_it_matters: If cost has kept you from trying a second AI tool alongside ChatGPT or Claude, Grok 4.5 is now a legitimately cheap option worth a test for research or writing, and it's built into the X app many small businesses already use.

categories.title: Tools & Tips for Creators and Small Biz

items.headline: Google Photos can now "remix" your videos into watercolor paintings, sketchbook art, and more with one tap
items.summary: Google Photos rolled out Video Remix on July 8, an AI tool that restyles any clip already in your library using templates like "paint it in dreamy watercolor" or "set it in a greenhouse." It's live now for Google AI Plus, Pro, and Ultra subscribers in the US and about a dozen other countries.
items.why_it_matters: This is a free-with-subscription way to turn ordinary phone footage into a stylized clip for Reels or Shorts without opening an editing app, worth a five-minute test on your next b-roll.

opportunity.angle: I tested Google Photos' new AI Video Remix tool on my b-roll so you don't have to

items.headline: Google Search Console can now show you how your Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube posts perform in Google Search
items.summary: Google rolled out "platform properties" in Search Console on July 7, a new report that lets you connect an Instagram, TikTok, X, or YouTube account and see which of your posts show up in Google Search and Discover, plus the exact search terms bringing people to them.
items.why_it_matters: Most creators have no idea Google even indexes their social posts, this free report shows you which of your Reels, Shorts, or TikToks are actually getting found through Google search, so you know which topics and titles are worth repeating.

opportunity.action_note: Go to Search Console, add a platform property for your most active channel (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, or X), and check which of your posts are already showing up in Google Search this week.
opportunity.angle: The free Google tool that shows which of your videos are getting found through search, not just the algorithm
